own_review_points: DATABASE_KEYS drops POSTGRESQL_DB which was previously supported via getOrDefault — backward-compat regression for existing compose files
USERNAME_KEYS keeps POSTGRESQL_USER and POSTGRES_USER, so the earlier-version human concern about breaking POSTGRESQL_USER is addressed in this final diff
POSTGRES_USERNAME is in fact a Bitnami-supported username variable (per the Bitnami env script the human cites)
Human-ref: original username tests (getUsernameWhenHasPostgresqlUser / getDatabaseWhenHasPostgresqlDb) should be kept and new ones added rather than renamed-away
DEFAULT_USERNAME constant and array/loop extraction are reasonable refactors
notes: v8 point 2 is the strongest finding and correct: removing POSTGRESQL_DB from DATABASE_KEYS is a real backward-compat regression (good). Point 4 (add a POSTGRESQL_DB regression test) is also good and overlaps the human test-coverage concern. But point 1 is WRONG/fabrication: it asserts POSTGRES_USERNAME and POSTGRES_DATABASE are 'not supported by any known PostgreSQL Docker image', yet the human reference's cited Bitnami source lists POSTGRES_USERNAME as supported (wrong -1, fab). Point 3 (array-vs-getOrDefault convention) cites sibling Environment classes outside the diff — trivial. Missed the human's username-side test-keeping points (:66/:81). net = 2 good - 1 wrong - 2 missed = -1.
